task_completion.description: Read the aircraft identifier from the maintenance task info file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/maintenance_task_info.txt', revealing the target aircraft identifier.

task_completion.description: Log into the Aerobridge admin interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Aerobridge Django administration dashboard at 'http://localhost:8000/admin/'.

task_completion.description: Fill out the new activity form with the required maintenance details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the 'Add Activity' (or similar) form in the Django admin, with the Name field set to '100-Hour Scheduled Inspection', Status set to 'Completed', Activity Type set to 'Maintenance', and the correct Aircraft selected.

task_completion.description: Successfully save the new maintenance activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Django admin activity list page with a green success banner indicating that the '100-Hour Scheduled Inspection' activity was added successfully.

task_completion.description: Create the activity confirmation text file with the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor showing the creation and saving of '/home/ga/Documents/activity_confirmation.txt' containing the exact text 'Activity logged for aircraft: <Identifier>', where <Identifier> matches the aircraft from the info file.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the Aerobridge system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical web browser (like Firefox or Chrome) navigating the Django admin interface, rather than terminal curl commands or Python scripts making HTTP requests.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely submitted the form rather than manipulating the DOM.
integrity.visual_evidence: The success message appears as a standard Django admin notification banner after a page reload, without browser developer tools open to spoof the text.

integrity.description: The confirmation file was actually saved to the filesystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the file is saved (no unsaved indicator), or a terminal command like 'cat' or 'ls' confirms the file exists at '/home/ga/Documents/activity_confirmation.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Aerobridge is a Django-based drone fleet management system. The admin interface is at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The expected activity name is exactly '100-Hour Scheduled Inspection'. The confirmation file must be created at '/home/ga/Documents/activity_confirmation.txt' and the info file is located at '/home/ga/Documents/maintenance_task_info.txt'.
